title: Effective communication
author: Priya
content:
A person's identity lies in their behaviour, their persona, and their social well-being. We say that never judge a book by its cover. Therefore, to work with your team, you have to be get acquainted with them to get familiar. Just knowing of them like 2 strangers won't be enough. Be with them and visualise their personal traits, their social behaviour, and the way they articulate and verbalise themselves in front of others.

No matter how much you earn, if you don't have the art to sell your values and knowledge to others. Your professional enigma can only be broken down if you brand yourself with your work ethic. No business could run if u can't buy others' trust on you. And no one would want to loosen the trust and reliance with your working brand because it takes years to build a good rapport of your work, but just a second to shred it. 

 The value attached to an item is not just the valuable goods and raw materials that are ingested in it, but also how to make it more qualitative among consumers, and that needs good communication skills to be more social and resourceful for the consumer. As our words are our melting pot of action to influence others and win their favour, to make our work count.

Hence, working on delivering your work output needs effective communication to maintain that accredited trust and faith that users have in your brand.

Some pin-points one has to gear up with for a compelling and incisive communication -

• Be simplistic but thoughtful -> Your ideas and notions must have a clear view that could drive your conversation towards a base point. Never have conflicting views that sound more obscure, unworthy of buying your client's proposal on your ideas.


• Have clear and concise ideation -> Figuratively, one has to be clear with the statistics and numbers when you want to present your facts engrained to give an exact outline of the work with validated numbers and figures. Brevity in your spoken words makes it easy to put forth your message for a more responsive outcome from the listener when sentences are captioned with 1 liner statement with usable words and phrases.


• Exemplify your ideas -> Always speak in a manner that makes your words sound more relatable and in-context to the main topic. Giving derivatives and facts supporting analogies can help in developing a good understanding of the base topic.


• Avoid any hype or exaggeration -> Your conversation should not sound overly hyperbolic and must exhibit realism and genuinity . Speaking too high of something can also undermine others' trust in you.


• Be mindful of hate speeches -> Refrain from using words that are more inciting and outrageous. Use of any illicit words that are more abusive and can lead to disharmony can account for lawlessness and anarchism. Hence, must be avoided.


• No superfluous words -> Speaking long sentences for an impactful conversation can actually end up adding redundancy and make your words sound more verbose. Always use words that make your conversation look natural with no detonating, extraneous words that have a resounding tone of superiority, but try adding up values with simple, neutral, and unprovoked words.


• Degree of comparison -> there shouldn't be any superlative comparison with other brands because putting yourself first to others could rank u down in keeping a good profile of your work in terms of your behaviour, right attitude, and your relationship with other global brands, even if professionally you're at the edge of your career.


• Your tonal voice or intonation -> Your tone of voice and your accent add a great impression of how well u articulate yourself and others can makeup a different opinion of your work if your postulates r countering u and others objectifing u because what u spoke is antiphrasis to what u suppose to say and that euphemism can be counter-productive to what u actually want your conversation to drive u through. How you admit your clients to buy your proposal entirely depends on your voice, your pitch, and how rightly u enunciate your words to others.


• Have a precise ball-mark figure -> precision with which u present your facts has to be accurate. All data points and statistical data give a curve of your regression and progression at every juncture in the workflow and a predictive analysis of your future roadmap.


• Have a clear thesis or reasoning -> Your idea, thoughts, and viewpoint must be strong and transparent. Any differing opinions of your work r subjected to mild disapproval from the client side, and any cognitive dissonance can rise up to adding negative feedback of your clients and partner group on your portfolio. And it reflects a very dark and obtuse side of your work when your stated facts r not racking up to the functional norms of your work and are incapable of making coherence with your partner and staff.
